content:
What clients and companies actually notice

4 min read
Just now

--

https://images.pexels.com/photos/3861972/pexels-photo-3861972.jpegI know what you’re thinking.
“How can anything be more valuable than coding for a developer?”
I used to think the same thing.
For years, I believed that the developer with the most algorithms, the deepest Python knowledge, and the cleanest architecture would automatically win.
Then I started working with startups.
Then enterprises.
Then clients.
And something became painfully obvious.
The highest-paid developers weren’t always the best coders.
In fact, some of the strongest programmers I knew stayed stuck in mid-level positions for years.
Meanwhile, developers with average coding skills kept getting promoted, leading teams, and landing consulting gigs that paid more than entire engineering salaries.
Why?
Because coding stopped being the bottleneck.
Everything around coding became the bottleneck.
Let’s talk about the seven skills that quietly became more valuable than writing code itself.
